info.name: Transact Bridge API Docs
info.description:
**Transact Bridge** APIs are REST JSON APIs. Our API accepts JSON requests and returns JSON responses, and uses standard HTTP response codes, authentication. You can use our APIs in the sandbox(testing) and production(live) environments. We provide two different API URLs and tokens for sandbox and production(live). You can use the API in sandbox mode, which doesn't affect your live data or interact with the banking networks. The API key you use to authenticate the request determines whether the request is in live mode or test mode. Our API doesn't support bulk updates. You can work on only one object per request. All the APIs are authenticated and cannot be utilized without sending proper authentication headers. # Authorization Sandbox mode secret keys have the prefix `tb_test_` and live mode secret keys have the prefix `tb_live_`. Your API keys carry many privileges, so be sure to keep them secure! Do not share your secret API keys in publicly accessible areas such as GitHub, client-side code, and so forth.

Environment

| **Type** | **URL** | | --- | --- | | Production API endpoint | [https://api.transactbridge.com](https://api.transactbridge.com) | | Production Partner Panel | [https://reseller.transactbridge.com/login](https://reseller.transactbridge.com/login) | | Production Customer Panel | [https://reseller.transactbridge.com/customer/login](https://reseller.transactbridge.com/customer/login) | | Sandbox API endpoint | [https://sandbox-api.transactbridge.com](https://sandbox-api.transactbridge.com) | | Sandbox Partner Panel | [https://sandbox.transactbridge.com/login](https://sandbox.transactbridge.com/login) | | Sandbox Customer Panel | [https://sandbox.transactbridge.com/customer/login](https://sandbox.transactbridge.com/customer/login) | We will provide the following credentials to the partner for accessing both APIs and the web panel once the email is provided by the partner. If the partner is creating an account directly on the sandbox environment, the partner would still need to email us so that we can configure the account for UAT purposes. Partners can use the panel to view all the transactions, subscriptions, sessions, and invoices, create new API keys, add team members, give permission to team members, whitelist server IPs for more security, configure callback URLs, etc.

Signature Generation

A signature is a mandatory header if using APIs. We are generating signatures using two details, the public **`API KEY`** and private **`SECRET KEY`**. **Sample Example**: Let partner Api key: tb_test_q47Wqf04e1sIZu22kVwWBrDncigOkIPN Let partner Secret key: tb_test_CT5u9ZeNMBEL884g6gP3 **Step 1**: Create a new string by concatenating the API key and secret key with a colon in between. In our sample, the string becomes "tb_test_q47Wqf04e1sIZu22kVwWBrDncigOkIPN:tb_test_CT5u9ZeNMBEL884g6gP3" **Step 2**: Create a signature using the above string using SHA-512 Algorithm. In our sample, the signature becomes 7ae85bcc8d0af6ceacd271da52d22d1fa091733b891ff133169ef959647f5d23232c6b6709150f1cbbabc5d2a815cb05b91448c3c3eabc9447343b689eba7606 Below are different examples for different languages ``` javascript const crypto = require('crypto'); function generateChecksumString(str) { return crypto.createHash('sha512').update(str).digest('hex'); } let signature = generateChecksumString("tb_test_q47Wqf04e1sIZu22kVwWBrDncigOkIPN:tb_test_CT5u9ZeNMBEL884g6gP3") console.log(signature); // will print below signature // 7ae85bcc8d0af6ceacd271da52d22d1fa091733b891ff133169ef959647f5d23232c6b6709150f1cbbabc5d2a815cb05b91448c3c3eabc9447343b689eba7606 ``` ``` php $shatest = ("tb_test_q47Wqf04e1sIZu22kVwWBrDncigOkIPN:tb_test_CT5u9ZeNMBEL884g6gP3"); $sha512test = hash("sha512",$shatest); echo $sha512test; // will print below signature // 7ae85bcc8d0af6ceacd271da52d22d1fa091733b891ff133169ef959647f5d23232c6b6709150f1cbbabc5d2a815cb05b91448c3c3eabc9447343b689eba7606 ``` ``` python import hashlib text = 'tb_test_q47Wqf04e1sIZu22kVwWBrDncigOkIPN:tb_test_CT5u9ZeNMBEL884g6gP3' m = hashlib.sha512(text.encode('UTF-8')) print(m.hexdigest()) # will print below signature # 7ae85bcc8d0af6ceacd271da52d22d1fa091733b891ff133169ef959647f5d23232c6b6709150f1cbbabc5d2a815cb05b91448c3c3eabc9447343b689eba7606 ``` **Step 3**: Pass the public **`API KEY`** and the generated signature in the headers like below.
Headers
Type
Description
Content-Type
String Mandatory
application/json
x-api-key
String Mandatory
Public API key provided by the platform to the partner.
x-signature
String Mandatory
A signature is created by the secret key provided by the platform to the partner. We use SHA-256 for creating signatures
# Payment Methods Transact Bridge has all the cards and alternative payment methods availabe in India. Below are the list with constraints in terms of minimum and maximum | **Pay Methods** | **Min Amt (INR**) | **Max Amt (INR)** | **Networks** | **Remarks** | | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | | UPI | 1 | 100,000 | PhonePe, PayTm, Google Pay, Bhim etc. | All 50+ UPI apps can be used. Also UPI on Credit cards are also available. | | Debit Cards | 1 | 100,000 | All domestically issued cards including VISA, Rupay, Master Card etc. | Max limits depends on issuing Bank limits as well | | Credit Cards | 1 | 1,000,000 | All domestically issued cards including VISA, Rupay, Master Card etc. | Max limits depends on issuing Bank limits as well | | Net Banking | 1 | 1,000,000 | 80+ Indian Domestic Banks | Max limits depends on issuing Bank limits as well | # Payment Flow Transact Bridge has two different customer payment flows, depending on the eligibility criteria the partner can choose from two: - **Redirection Flow**: In this flow, the customer will be redirected first from the partner's website to a domain hosted by Transact Bridge where the customer will enter the requisite information (depending on what compliances) to make payment, and after making payment the customer will be redirected back to the partner's website. The redirection URL will be provided in response to the [Create Session API](#d4607171-457b-4d24-8277-6d9a79a997dd) and [Create Subscription API](#307dc665-ae10-4e43-8853-5b7f04961825) requests. - **Iframe Flow**: In this flow, the customer is not being redirected but the checkout view of Transact Bridge will be visible on the partner's website as an overlay or embedded solution. The customer will enter the requisite information (depending on what compliances) to make payment, and after making payment the iframe will be closed with events being pushed to the partner's website. The redirection URL through which the iframe can be invoked will be provided in response to the [Create Session API](#d4607171-457b-4d24-8277-6d9a79a997dd) and [Create Subscription API](#307dc665-ae10-4e43-8853-5b7f04961825) requests. The partner can find the iframe script and other relevant documents using these links for [script](https://cdn.transactbridge.com/scripts/iframe.js), [readme file](https://cdn.transactbridge.com/scripts/README.md), and [sample HTML file](https://cdn.transactbridge.com/scripts/testIframe.html). # Request/Response All APIs are JSON-based and therefore only accept JSON in the request body. We have pagination for those APIs which are for getting a list of objects.

Request Body

Following are some of the common parameters that the request body accepts.
Parameter
Type
Description
filter
Object
In getting a list of Objects API filter parameter allows us to narrow down the results. Each API can have a different filter parameters.
page
Number
Used to paginate if the API is of Get a list of Objects.
sort
Object
In getting a list of Objects API sort parameter allows us to get a sorted result. Each API can have a different sort parameters.
fromDate
Date
The date format used is
yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss
For Ex. 2023-10-19 18:30:00

Response Body

All the responses will contain these 4 parameters `status`, `sub_status`, `msg`, `data` . Other parameters are on a need basis.
Parameter
Type
Description
status
String
Enum:success, error
This is the final status of the API, if the status is not successful then consider that the API did not run successfully. You can check msg parameter to understand what went wrong.
sub_status
String
Used for internal purposes only
error_code
String
This parameter is only available when status in the response body is error.
msg
String
To understand the error if the request was not successful.
data
Array or Object
Contains the response data of the request.
maxPageSize
Number
The maximum number of data objects that are returned in the Get All requests.
hasMore
Boolean
If the Get All request has more data objects then the value is true. Use page parameter in the request to get the next data objects.
# Encryption Encryption is used to send sensitive data like credit card and debit card details in the S2S API. We use "RSA (Padding: RSA_PKCS1_OAEP_PADDING)" algorithm to encrypt the data string using a key. To use this module the parnter must be PCI-certified. There is no need to integrate this if the parnter is not using [Create Payment Request](https://docs.transactbridge.com/#757c3604-04ea-494c-9d61-aa30a15373e7). ``` javascript // Crypto module is used to encrypt a data string const crypto = require('crypto'); // data is the payload string that needs to be encrypted // publicKey is provided to the partner by Transact Bridge function encryptRsa(publicKey, data) { try { const encryptMe = crypto.publicEncrypt( { key: publicKey, padding: crypto.constants.RSA_PKCS1_OAEP_PADDING, }, Buffer.from(data), ); let hash = encryptMe.toString('base64'); return hash; } catch (error) { throw error; } } ``` # Definitions

Transaction Types

Different types of transactions represent different use cases.
Types
Description
DEPOSIT
This is a credit transaction and will be credited only when the transaction is in status SUCCESS.
REFUND
This is a debit transaction for the refund raised by the partner.
CHARGEBACK
This is a debit transaction for the refund raised by the bank against dissatisfaction.

Billing Session Status

These billing session statuses represent the state the session is currently in.
Types
Description
CREATED
A session has been created but the link has not been used by the customer to initiate the payment window.
STARTED
When the customer opens the payment link and proceeds with accepting TnC.
CLOSED
Payment has been successfully done by the customer and an invoice has been created.
EXPIRED
In case of non-payment, the link will be expired after a configured interval. Partners can pass the expiry date at the time of session creation. The default expiry date is 24 hours from the time of the creation of the session.
REJECTED
This status needs to be enabled by the Admin Team, once done, if a customer clicks on "Back to Shopping" on the payment page then the billing session will be marked REJECTED and no further attempts will be allowed.

Subscription Status

These subscription statuses represent the state the subscription is currently in.
Types
Description
PENDING
A subscription has been created, the link is active and the customer can pay on the link to start the subscription in TRIAL or ACTIVE status
PENDING_EXPIRED
When the subscription has crossed the pendingExpire minutes configured during the creation of the subscription, the status is changed to PENDING_EXPIRED. This is a terminal state and the customer cannot attempt a payment after this.
TRIAL
Subscription is in trial mode and depending on the trialSettings post completion of trial auto debit may occur or may not.
ACTIVE
Subscription has been activated by adding a payment method for postpaid subscriptions or paying in full for prepaid subscriptions.
PAUSED
Subscription has been paused by the system configured by the partner using reAttemptConfig.
Subscription can also be paused directly by the partner
EXPIRED
CANCELLED
The subscription has been canceled by the end customer using the customer panel. If partialCancel was allowed in the subscription, then the customer would be able to cancel on an immediate basis as well. The subscription can also be cancelled directly by the partner or by the system depending on the configuration of reAttemptConfig.
FAILED
The subscription is in unknown status due to unforeseen reasons.

Invoice Status

These invoice statuses represent the actual status of the invoice.
Status
Txn Sub Status
Deposit Desc
DRAFT
CREATED
The invoice has just been created and there is pending approval from the partner.
APPROVED
N/A
The invoice has been approved by the partner and we will try to auto-debit the customer on the due date.
DUE
N/A
The invoice is in due state and the customer can pay on the invoice. If there is a mandate available, we will try for auto-debit as well.
OVERDUE
N/A
The invoice could not be paid within the due time and is not in the overdue state. Customers can still attempt to make the payment on the invoice.
PAID
CAPTURED
The invoice has been successfully paid and is ready to be sent to the customer.
Note: Billing Session Flow will only have invoices in this state from the start.
PAID
CHARGEBACK
There has been a chargeback request on the invoice and is in the processing state.
PAID
CHARGEBACK-COMPLETED
The chargeback has been processed and a credit note has been created.
PAID
REFUND
There has been a refund request on the invoice and is in the processing state.
PAID
REFUND-COMPLETED
The refund has been processed and a credit note has been created.
CANCELLED
N/A
The invoice has been marked canceled by the partner
VOID
N/A
The invoice has been marked void by the partner.

Deposit Transaction Status

These transaction statuses represent the actual status of the transaction.
Status
Txn Sub Status
Deposit Desc
PENDING
INITIATED
The transaction has just been created and there has been a payment attempt on it.
PROCESSING
N/A
A payment attempt has been made on the transaction. It is an intermediary state until we get a confirmation from the acquirer.
CANCELLED
VOID
The transaction has been cancelled and no further payment attempt can be made. This is a terminal state.
FAILED
VOID
A payment attempt was made on the transaction but due to any reason, the transaction was failed by the acquirer. To know more check the txStatusCode value.
FAILED
REJECTED
A payment attempt was made but the payment was rejected either by the customer of by the bank due to insufficient funds.
SUCCESS
CAPTURED
The payment attempt on the transaction has been successful and a successful debit has been made on the customer's account.
**Note:** A transaction is treated as successful only if the status is **`SUCCESS`** and txSubStatus is **`CAPTURED`**. The transactions which were accepted as chargeback will have the status **`SUCCESS`** and txSubStatus as **`CHARGEBACK`**

Refund Transaction Status

These transaction statuses represent the actual status of the transaction.
Status
Deposit Desc
PENDING
A refund has been created by the partner. Once we acknowledge the status will be changed to PROCESSING.
PROCESSING
The refund has been passed over to the bank. We are waiting for the status update from the bank.
SUCCESS
The refund has been successfully done and the payment reached the end customer.
FAILED
Due to some reason, the refund could not be completed.
CANCELLED
The refund request has been canceled by the partner.

Subscription Frequency

A subscription is a recurring billing mechanism which requires the billing cycle that is the frequency at which invoice has to be raised and subsequent auto-debit from customer has to be made. Below are the frequencies that are allowed.
Frequency
Remark
DAIL
For billing that requires daily auto-debit.
Note: Even though the billing is daily, the invoice needs to be generated 1-2 days prior depending on the payment method.
WEEK
For billing that requires weekly / 7-day based auto-debit.
MNTH
For billing that requires monthly auto-debit.
BIMN
For billing that requires bi-monthly auto-debit.
QURT
For billing that requires quaterly auto-debit.
MIAN
For billing that requires semi-annully that is 6 months based auto-debit.
YEAR
For billing that requires yearly based auto-debit.
ONDM
For billing that requires on demand based auto-debit.
Note: This is not available in India as of now due to regulatory compliances.

Validators

| **Parameter** | **Regex** | **Description** | | --- | --- | --- | | AllowedString | /^\[a-z\\d\\s\\-/.()_:\]+$/i | Allowed: alphabets, numerals, space, dot, slash, hyphen, parenthesis | | Address | /^\[a-z\\d\\s\\-_.'#&()=/{},\[\\\]\]+$/i; | Allowed: alphabets, numerals, space, hyphens, underscore, ampersand, dot, apostrophe, hash, parenthesis, equal, slash, curly brackets, square brackets, backslash. | | MultiLineDescription | /^(\[a-z\\d\\s\\-/+/,/.()@\]+ | )+$/i | | ReferenceId | /^\[a-z\\d\\-/.()_:\]+$/i | Allowed: alphabets, numerals, dots, slash, hyphen, parenthesis, underscore, colon | | MongoId | /^\[0-9a-fA-F\]{24}$/; | | # UAT Testing Transact Bridge provides UAT testing credentials for UPI, Credit Card, Debit Card, and Net banking to test the created transactions and check end-to-end flows of payments. We also provide test details for PAN (Tax Identity in India) and GST verification as well. **Note**: The lower limit is defined as Rs. 1/- and the upper limit is defined as Rs. 1000/-

Test PAN & GST Details

| **Parameter** | **Value** | **Description** | | --- | --- | --- | | PAN | ABCDE1234F | You can use this to verify PAN if required or prompted in the UAT for testing purpose. | | GST | 24AAACJ3770E2ZV | You can use this to verify GST if required or prompted in the UAT for testing purpose. |

Test UPI Details for One-time

| **Parameter** | **Value** | **Description** | | --- | --- | --- | | vpa | tbridgesuccess@upi | Using this as the UPI, the transaction will get auto success in 60 seconds | | vpa | tbridgefailedrefund@upi | Using this UPI, the transaction will get auto success in 60 seconds.
If a refund is raised on the invoice of this particular session, it will fail in the next 60 seconds. | | vpa | tbridgesuccessrefund@upi | Using this UPI, the transaction will get auto success in 60 seconds.
If a refund is raised on the invoice of this particular session, it will get success in the next 60 seconds. | | vpa | tbridgefailure@upi | Using this as the UPI, the transaction will fail in 60 seconds | **Note**: In the sandbox enter the above VPA/UPI Id and click on verify. Once it is successful initiate payment and wait for 90 seconds, the payment will be automatically successful.

Error Codes

These error codes represent specific errors. This parameter only comes when there is a specific error and not in every case.
Error Code
Description

03
The amount is not in the minimum and maximum bounds for a withdrawal Transaction
07
The risk threshold exceeded for this payment
08

09
API authentication is incorrect
10
No payment method is configured for this account
11
Reference ID already exists
13
The entered PIN code is not correct
14
Invalid GST number
15
GST service is unavailable right now
16
GST number is inactive
18
The entered payment method is not active for this account

The amount is greater than the maximum allowed.

Tx Status Code

These TX status codes represent specific acquirer messages. This parameter only comes when there is a specific error and not in every case.
Tx Status Code
Description
100
The transaction did not reach the bank in time and timed out.
101
Transaction cancelled by User
102
Transaction cancelled due to insufficient balance
103
Transaction failed at acquirer due to an unknown reason
104
Transaction failed at user due to unknown reason
105
Transaction failed due to not making payment in due time
106
Transaction failed because of an invalid UPI/account number
500
Transaction failed due bank server down

Data Format Errors

These errors are specific messages when the data is not allowed by the validator.
Tx Status Code
Description
multiComment
Allowed characters: alphabets, numerical, space, dot, slash, hyphen, parenthesis, URL links
Maximum Length: 256 characters
multiLineDescription
Allowed characters: alphabets, numerical, space, dot, slash, hyphen, parenthesis, at
Maximum Length: 256 characters
onlyAlphaNumeric
Allowed Characters: alphabets, numerical
Maximum Length: 256 characters
gstNumberFormat
Allowed Characters: alphabets, numerical;
It is a format of alphanumeric strings described by the govt of India.
Maximum Length: 15 characters
referenceId
Allowed characters: alphabets, numerical, dot, hyphen, parenthesis, colon, underscore
Maximum Length: 256 characters
DateObject
Allowed format yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss
For Ex. 2023-10-19 18:30:00

item.name: Sessions

request.description:
This API is used to create a unique payment link with a specific amount, that can be shared with a customer. Once the user pays the requested amount through the selected payment channel, the partner gets a webhook call for a successful credit of the same session. A session can accommodate multiple transactions. Each transaction is considered an attempt to make payments with valid credentials. A customer can try to make a payment using different payment methods in different payment attempts. Using the [Get Session API](https://), all the transactions created during a session can also be found and each transaction status can be found using [Get Transaction API](https://).

Request Parameters

Parameter
Type
Description
email
String Optional
Customer's email. Require this to send an invoice.
clientReferenceId
String Optional
Customer's reference id that the partner can pass to map it to the customer on Transact Bridge panel. If email is not passed in the request body, the customer must enter the email on the product checkout page.
phoneNo
String Optional
The customer's Indian phone number in the format of
+91-XXXXXXXX
theme
String Optional
Enum: LIGHT, DARK, AUTO
Checkout renders accordingly to choosen theme.
taxIdentity
String Optional
PAN number of the customer
gstIdentity
String Optional
If the customer is business type then GST can be used so that the business can get input credits.
customerId
String Optional
Validator: MongoId
If the customer is already created, use customerId and then email will be optional
name
String Optional
Validator: AllowedString
Name of the customer
quoteCurrCode
String Mandatory
Enum: USD, EURO, INR
Fiat currency for which request needs to be initiated.
returnUrl
String Optional
This URL is needed to redirect the user after the session is completed. The end customer will be redirected to this URL with the status of the session.
pendingUrl
String Optional
This URL is needed to redirect the user after the session is in CREATED or STARTED status
failedUrl
String Optional
This URL is needed to redirect the user after the session is in EXPIRED status
successUrl
String Optional
This URL is needed to redirect the user after the session is in CLOSED status
referenceId
String Optional
Validator: ReferenceId
This is a partner ID to map a billing session with their generated ID. This cannot be the same for two sessions.
userTaxId
String Optional
TaxId created by the partner. This allows us to tax the product correctly. For example: Partners can have two different userTaxId one for 18% GST and the other for 5% GST.
shipTo
Object Optional
Shipping Details of the Customer
billTo
Object Optional
Billing Details of the customer for the invoice.
sameAsBilling
Boolean Optional
To make the shipping address the same as the billing address
payMethod
Array of String Optional
Enum: UPI, NB, CC, DC
If the payMethod parameter is sent by the partner then on the payment page for the customer only that payment method will be visible to the user.
redirectionType
String Optional
Enum: SAME_PAGE, NEW_PAGE
Depending on the redirection type value, the user will either be redirected to a new page for payment or to the same window of the product page. Default value is NEW_PAGE.
items
Array of Object Mandatory
Items the customer wants to buy.
discount
Object Optional
This parameter can be used to reduce the total cart pay amount.
expiryDate
Date Optional
The UTC date at which the session link will expire. If this is not passed by the partner, then the link will expire after 24 hours. This is the expiry of the link if a customer attempts the payment at the last minute, then the link will not expire until all the payment attempts transactions are not in the terminal state.
Note: Minimum 10 minutes in the future will be accepted as a valid expiryDate from the time of session creation.
invoice
Object Optional
Invoice configuration for the product sold in this billing session.
serviceStart
Date Optional
If the product is for a specific period, then the invoice created for the customer will have this parameter.
serviceEnd
Date Optional
If the product is for a specific period, then the invoice created for the customer will have this parameter.
meta
Object Optional
To add meta params in the session. They will be passed in the webhook and query API as well.
referrerUrl
String Optional
To add the checkout original URL from which customer got redirected to Transact Bridge checkout page
enableMandatoryPopup
Boolean Optional
If this is set to be true then the customer on the checkout page will be first shown to add remaining mandatory inputs like name etc.
**Note:** 1. If `sameAsBilling` the parameter value is set as `true` then the invoice will have the same shipping and billing address. 2. `pendingUrl` , `successUrl` , `failedUrl` are all mandatory if `returnUrl` is not passed. 3. One of `email` or `clientReferenceId` is a mandatory parameter. > **`billTo`** & **`shipTo`** Object Parameters | **Parameters** | **Type** | **Description** | | --- | --- | --- | | name | String `Optional`
Validator: `AllowedString` | Name of the customer | | line1 | String `Optional`
Validator: `Address` | Address Line 1 of the customer | | line2 | String `Optional`
Validator: `Address` | Address Line 2 of the customer | | city | String `Optional`
Validator: `AllowedString` | City of the customer | | postalCode | String `Optional` | Pin code of the customer. Only Indian codes are accepted. | | state | String `Optional`
Enum: `State` | State of the customer address | | country | String `Optional`
Enum: **`INDIA`** | Country of the customer. Only India is accepted right now. | > **`items`** Array of Object Parameters | **Parameters** | **Type** | **Description** | | --- | --- | --- | | productReferenceId | String `Optional`
Validator: `referenceId` | If the product is already created use the refereneId of that product. | | name | String `Mandatory`
Validator: `AllowedString` | Name of the product the customer wants to buy. | | quantity | Number `Mandatory` | Number of products the customer wants to buy. | | amount | Number `Mandatory` | Price of the product. | | description | String `Optional`
Validator: `MultiLineDescription` | Description of the product. | | hsnCode | String `Optional` | If the product is physical then HSN code is mandatory | | shippable | Boolean `Optional`
Default: `false` | If the product is physical pass this param as `true`. | **Note:** 1. Description in the items object allows HTML syntax for customer view. Use so that the description on the product page is better from a UI perspective. We break and show the description like unlisted items. 2. If `productReferenceId` is passed then name `hsnCode` and `shippable` is not mandatory. > `meta` Object Parameters | **Parameters** | **Type** | **Description** | | --- | --- | --- | | param1 | String `Optional` | String with max length of 256 characters. | | param2 | String `Optional` | String with max length of 256 characters. | | param3 | String `Optional` | String with max length of 256 characters. | > **`invoice`** Object Parameters | **Parameters** | **Type** | **Description** | | --- | --- | --- | | issueDate | Date `Optional` | Issue date of the invoice | | dueDate | Date `Optional` | Due date of the invoice | > **`discount`** Object Parameters | **Parameters** | **Type** | **Description** | | --- | --- | --- | | name | String `Optional` | This will be shown on the checkout page as the description of the discount being applied. | | quoteAmountOff | Number `Mandatory` | Discount value that is being applied on the cart value. |

Response Parameters

Parameter
Type
Description
status
String
The response from the API
sub_status
String
Used for internal purposes only
data
Object
Contains the sessionId and URL.
**Note:** **`returnUrl`** will be a **GET** redirection with status, billingSessionId, and referenceId values passed in the query params. Partner can then use [Get Session API](https://docs.transactbridge.com/#49db9313-bb0c-4732-a59d-b9ce0fec6217) to get the full details of that session.

item.description:
A payment link is a shareable URL that will take your customers to a hosted payment page. A payment link can be shared and used multiple times. The session is considered closed once the payment is done successfully. Before that customer can retry any number of times to make the payment before the session expires (configured by the partner).

Partners can use the [Get Session API](https://docs.transactbridge.com/#49db9313-bb0c-4732-a59d-b9ce0fec6217) to track the status of the session. Invoices are created once the payment is successful and is sent to the end customer.

The payments once done by the customer are reflected instantly in the panel and webhook/callback events are also pushed to the configured URL for all the activities done in the session and also for invoices.

Since the payments are browser-based, the partner needs to set redirect URLs to where the customer will be redirected once the payment is done.

item.name: Payment

request.description:
This API is used to create a unique payment request with a specific amount. Once the user pays the requested amount through the selected payment channel, the partner gets a webhook call for a successful credit of the same deposit. To send the debit card and credit card details, the partner would require to use encryption and then send encrypted data. Read the [encryption documentation](https://docs.transactbridge.com/#encryption) to understand how to use it. Each transaction status can be found using [Get Transaction API](https://). To verify if the encryption is being done correctly in the sandbox environment (Do not work in Production Environment), use [Encryption Testing API](https://docs.transactbridge.com/#cdb602e4-c8e8-4cdd-9f36-3ca8e921418c).

Request Parameters

Parameter
Type
Description
email
String Optional
Customer's email. Require this to send an invoice.
phoneNo
String Optional
The customer's Indian phone number in the format of
+91-XXXXXXXX
name
String Optional
Validator: AllowedString
Name of the customer
returnUrl
String Mandatory
This URL is needed to redirect the user after the session is completed. The end customer will be redirected to this URL with the status of the session.
referenceId
String Mandatory
Validator: ReferenceId
This is a partner ID to map a billing session with their generated ID. This cannot be the same for two sessions.
payMethod
Array of String Mandatory
Enum: UPI, NB, CC, DC
If the payMethod parameter is sent by the partner then on the payment page for the customer only that payment method will be visible to the user.
meta
Object Optional
To add meta params in the session. They will be passed in the webhook and query API as well.
**Note**: For different **`payMethod`** there are different params to send the correspoding values mandatory for that **`payMethod`**. Please refer to the examples to understand what other parameters can be sent. > `meta` Object Parameters | **Parameters** | **Type** | **Description** | | --- | --- | --- | | param1 | String `Optional` | String with max length of 256 characters. | | param2 | String `Optional` | String with max length of 256 characters. | | param3 | String `Optional` | String with max length of 256 characters. |

Response Parameters

Parameter
Type
Description
status
String
The response from the API
sub_status
String
Used for internal purposes only
data
Object
Contains the sessionId and URL.
**Note:** 1. For UPI Collect request there will be no redirection URL. 2. For UPI Intent reqeust there will be a intent URL provided in the data object. 3. In all other payment methods (NB, CC, DC) there will be a redirection URL proviced in the data object.

item.name: Subscriptions

request.description:
This API is used to create a unique payment link for a subscription, that can be shared with a customer. Once the user pays the requested amount through the selected payment channel, the partner gets a webhook call for a successful credit and subscription. At the start, the subscription is in **`PENDING`** state, which means the customer can pay to activate the subscription. If the customer fails to make payment before the date derived from **`pendingExpire`** then the subscription is moved into **`PENDING_EXPIRED`** which is a terminal state after which no payment can be made on the same subscription. If a subscription is created with **`partialCancel`** set to be **`true`** then only the subscription can be canceled in the middle of the billing cycle. If **`partialCancel`** is set to be **`false`** then only **`canelAtEnd`** can be used to cancel the subscription at the end of the billing cycle. A subscription can accommodate multiple transactions. Each transaction is considered an attempt to make payments with valid credentials. A customer can try to make a payment using different payment methods in different payment attempts. Using the [Get Session API](https://), all the transactions created during a session can also be found and each transaction status can be found using [Get Transaction API](https://).

Request Parameters

Parameter
Type
Description
customerId
String Optional
Validator: MongoId
If the customer is already created use customerId and then email is optional parameter
email
String Optional
Customer's email. Require this to send an invoice.
clientReferenceId
String Optional
Customer's reference id that the partner can pass to map it to the customer on Transact Bridge panel. If email is not passed in the request body, the customer must enter the email on the product checkout page.
phoneNo
String Optional
The customer's Indian phone number in the format of
+91-XXXXXXXX
taxIdentity
String Optional
PAN number of the customer
gstIdentity
String Optional
If the customer is business type then GST can be used so that the business can get input credits.
name
String Optional
Name of the customer
quoteCurrCode
String Mandatory
Enum: USD, EURO, INR
Fiat currency for which request needs to be initiated.
maxQuoteAmount
String Mandatory
This is the amount in the same denomination as quoteCurrCode. This is the maximum value for which we can use the mandate for auto debit the amount. This amount will be inclusive of GST/VAT. Please configure it accordingly.
Note: UPI has a maximum limit of ₹ 15,000 per mandate. Cards have a limit of ₹ 500,000 per mandate.
returnUrl
String Mandatory
This URL is needed to redirect the user after the session is completed.
referenceId
String Optional
This is a partner-generated ID to map a subscription. Partners can use this to query the subscriptions as well. This cannot be the same for two subscriptions.
userTaxId
String Optional
We will create a userTaxId for the partner. This allows us to tax the product correctly. For example: Partners can have two different userTaxId one for 18% GST and the other for 5% GST.
payMethod
Array of String Optional
Enum: UPI, NB, CC, DC
If payMethod parameter is sent by the partner then on the payment page for the customer only that payment method will be visible to user.
redirectionType
String Optional
Enum: SAME_PAGE, NEW_PAGE
Default: SAME_PAGE
Depending on the redirection type value, the user will either be redirected to a new page for payment or to the same window of the product page. Default value is NEW_PAGE.
frequency
String Mandatory
Enum: DAIL, WEEK, MNTH, BIMN, QURT, MIAN, YEAR, ONDM
Frequency of the subscription renewal period.
subscriptionBegin
Date Optional
Date of the start of the subscription period
subscriptionEnd
Date Optional
Date of the end of the subscription period
description
String Optional
To set the subscription view
collectionMechanism
String Optional
Enum: AUTOPAY
Only AUTOPAY is allowed right now.
billAtStart
Boolean Optional
Default true
If this is true, then the subscription is prepaid based subscription and allows to deduction of money before providing the product to the end customer and generating the invoice at the start of the subscription period. If this is false then it is a postpaid subscription which creates an invoice at the end of the subscription period.
pendingExpire
String Optional
Number of minutes after which the status of the subscription if PENDING (Customer did not pay) it becomes PENDING_EXPIRED which is a terminal state and now customers cannot pay on the same subscription.
maxUnpaidInvoices
String Optional
String 0
Max unpaid invoices allowed for the subscription. For example, if maxUnpaidInvoices is set to 2 then after 2 unpaid invoices the subscription status will be converted from ACTIVE to status based from postMaxUnpaidInvoices
postMaxUnpaidInvoices
String Optional
Enum: PENDING, PENDING_EXPIRED, PAUSED, EXPIRED, CANCELLED
Default PAUSED
Subscription status after maxUnpaidInvoices the threshold is crossed by the customer.
Default Value: PAUSED
dayUntillDue
String Optional
Default 1
Period of time from the issue date after which DUE invoice is converted into OVERDUE status
billingDateAnchor
String Optional
It allows the partner to configure the billing cycle from a fixed date. If not passed then the subscription period will start from the day the customer pays for it. For Example: if the frequency is MNTH (monthly) and billingDateAnchor is set to 2, this means that the invoice will be created on every 2nd day of each month.
partialCancel
Boolean Optional
Default false
If the subscription is allowed to be canceled before the subscription renewal the amount payable is charged on a pro-rate basis. This setting is only applicable if the subscription is of billAtStart is false.
trialSettings
Object Optional
Configuration of the trial mechanism
maxUnpausedInvoiceDueCount
Object Optional
Maximum number of unpaid invoices count allowed after that the subscription is PAUSED by the system. For Ex: If a subscription has been paused due to 3 non payment of invoices and this parameter is set to be 2 then once the customer pays 1 invoice the subscription will be re activated.
reAttemptConfig
Array of Object Optional
To configure how the reattempt for failed invoices be processed.
shipTo
Object Optional
Shipping Details of the Customer
billTo
Object Mandatory
Billing Details of the customer
sameAsBilling
Boolean Optional
To make the shipping address the same as the billing address
items
Array of Object Mandatory
Items the customer wants to buy.
meta
Object Optional
To add meta params in the subscription. They will be passed in the webhook and query API as well.
**Note:** 1. If `sameAsBilling` the parameter value is set as `true` then `shipTo` is not mandatory and the invoice will have the same shipping and billing address. 2. `pendingUrl` , `successUrl` , `failedUrl` are all mandatory if `returnUrl` is not passed. 3. One of `email` or `clientReferenceId` is a mandatory parameter. > `meta` Object Parameters | **Parameters** | **Type** | **Description** | | --- | --- | --- | | param1 | String `Optional` | String with max length of 256 characters. | | param2 | String `Optional` | String with max length of 256 characters. | | param3 | String `Optional` | String with max length of 256 characters. | > **`billTo`** & **`shipTo`** Object Parameters | **Parameters** | **Type** | **Description** | | --- | --- | --- | | name | String `Optional` | Name of the customer | | line1 | String `Optional` | Address Line 1 of the customer | | line2 | String `Optional` | Address Line 2 of the customer | | city | String `Optional` | City of the customer | | postalCode | String `Optional` | Pin code of the customer. Only Indian codes are accepted. | | state | String `Optional` | State of the customer address | | country | String `Optional`
Enum: **`INDIA`** | Country of the customer. Only India is accepted right now. | > **`items`** Array of Object Parameters | **Parameters** | **Type** | **Description** | | --- | --- | --- | | productReferenceId | String `Mandatory`
Validator: `referenceId` | If the product is already created use the refereneId of that product. | | name | String `Mandatory` | Name of the product the customer wants to buy. | | quantity | Number `Mandatory` | Number of products the customer wants to buy. | | amount | Number `Mandatory` | Price of the product. | | prorate | Number `Mandatory` | If the invoice needs to be partially paid the total amount will be calculated using prorate on day consumed basis. | | description | String `Optional` | Description of the product. | **Note:** 1. Description in the items object allows HTML syntax for customer view. Use so that the description on the product page is better from a UI perspective. We break and show the description like unlisted items. 2. If `productReferenceId` is passed then `name` is not mandatory. > **`reAttemptConfig`** Array of Object Parameters | **Parameters** | **Type** | **Description** | | --- | --- | --- | | days | String `Mandatory` | No of days after which reattempt to be done | | attemptType | String `Mandatory`
Enum: **`try_again`**, **`subscription_active-invoice_unpaid`**, **`subscription_paused-invoice_unpaid`**, **`subscription_expire-invoice_unpaid`**, **`subscription_expire-invoice_cancelled`** | Depending on the enum passed post reattempt flow is configured.
**`try_again`**: Will configure the system to make a reattempt.
Other than **`try_again`** are terminal states that will change the status of the subscription and invoice according to the enum configured. | **Note:** Description in the items object allows HTML syntax for customer view. Use so that the description on the product page is better from a UI perspective. We break and show the description like unlisted items. > **`trialSettings`** Object Parameters | **Parameters** | **Type** | **Description** | | --- | --- | --- | | freeTrialExpire | Date `Mandatory` | Date in the future at which the trial should expire. | | needPayMethod | Boolean `Optional` | If a valid payment method with the mandate is required to activate the trial. | | reminderBeforeTrialEnd | String `Optional` | Number of days before the **`freeTrialExpire`** to send an email to remind the customer of the upcoming debit to convert **`trial`** to **`active`** subscription. The default value is "2". |

Response Parameters

Parameter
Type
Description
status
String
The response from the API
sub_status
String
Used for internal purposes only
data
Object
Contains the subscriptionId and URL.
**Note:** **`returnUrl`** will be a **GET** redirection with status, subscriptionId, and referenceId values passed in the query parameters. Partners can then use the Get Subscription API to get the full details of that session.

request.description:
This API is used to cancel an **`ACTIVE`** or **`PAUSED`** subscription. Canceling a subscription works differently for the Prepaid and Postpaid billing cycles. If **`cancelAtEnd`** is used, the subscription will remain in **`ACTIVE`** state until the end of the billing cycle. At the end of the billing cycle, the subscription will be auto-canceled and no new invoice will be raised. if cancelNow is used, it will change the status from **`ACTIVE`** to **`CANCELLED`** then only. For `Prepaid`, **`cancelNow`** is not allowed since we cannot rever the amount paid at the start of the cycle, therefore always use **`cancelAtEnd`** it to cancel a **`ACTIVE`** subscription. For `Postpaid`, **`cancelNow`** and **`cancelAtEnd`** can be used. If **`cancelNow`** is used, it will raise a pro-rate-based invoice and will try to debit the amount using auto-debit mandate if available. In the subscription analytics, this is considered as if the user has requested to drop out of the subscription.

Request Parameters

| **Parameters** | **Type** | **Description** | | --- | --- | --- | | subscriptionId | String Validator
Validator: `MongoId` | Subscription Id of the subscription for which update is needed. | | cancelAtEnd | Boolean `Optional` | Subscriptions will be canceled at the end of the cycle.
If **`cancelAtEnd`** is used to cancel a subscription, in response to this API, the subscription remains in the **`ACTIVE`** state and **`cancelAtEnd`** the parameter is changed to **`true`**. | | cancelNow | Boolean `Optional` | Subscriptions will be canceled at the
Note: Subscriptions that are created with **`billAtStart`**: **`true`** cannot be canceled in the middle of the cycle and can only be canceled using **`cancelAtEnd`**. | **Note:** One of the parameter **`cancelNow`** or **`cancelAtEnd`** is mandatory to pass in the request body.

Response Parameters

Parameter
Type
Description
status
String
The response from the API
sub_status
String
Used for internal purposes only
data
Object
Contains the updated subscription details.

request.description:
This API is used to pause an **`ACTIVE`** subscription. This API updates the status of the subscription to **`PAUSED`**. Paused subscriptions do not incur billing and the invoice will be created according to a **`prorate`** basis at the end of the cycle. Once **`PAUSED`**, a subscription can be **`UNPAUSED`** to reinstate the billing. If the subscription is marked **`PAUSED`** and then **`UNPAUSED`** again in the same billing cycle, it would not be considered **`PAUSED`** at all and the billing cycle will be considered to be used fully.

Validator: `ReferenceId` | Subscription referenceId that was passed during the creation of the subscription. | **Note:** One of the parameter **`subscriptionId`** or **`referenceId`** is mandatory to pass in the request body.

Response Parameters

Type
Description
status
String
The response from the API
sub_status
String
Used for internal purposes only
data
Object
Contains the updated subscription details.

item.description:
A subscription allows you to make recurring payments for a product. Subscriptions can be prepaid or postpaid. For creating a subscription a lot more information will be needed to configure the recurring and manage the billing cycle for a subscription.

A subscription link is a shareable URL that will take your customers to a hosted payment page. A subscription link can be shared but needs to be paid before it expires (configured by the partner).

The subscriptions once subscribed by the customer are reflected instantly in the panel and webhook/callbacks are also pushed to the configured URLs for all the activities done in the subscription.

Since the payments are browser-based, the partner needs to set redirect URLs to where the customer will be redirected once the payment is done.

### Subscription Flow

Transact Bridge has a very dynamic system that allows different types of billing cycles depending on the demands. Subscriptions can be widely categorized into two types namely `PREPAID` and `POSTPAID`.

##### Prepaid Billing Flow:

This type of billing is for those platforms which require the customer to pay the subscription amount in advance, and once the payment is received the services are available to the customer for consumption. This is generally used for those billing cycles that have fixed plans and are independent of resource-based billing. An example of this is Netflix, where the customer pays the subscription amount at the start of using the platform. On the renewal of the subscription, the auto-debit is also done before the access to the services.

To use this type of billing flow, the Partner needs to pass **`billAtStart`**: **`true`** when creating the subscription.

##### Postpaid Billing Flow:

This type of billing is for those platforms that require the customer to pay the subscription amount after the services have been used. This is generally used for those billing cycles that have metered billing and are independent of resources used during the billing cycle. An example of this is Amazon Web Services, where the customer pays the subscription amount at the end of the billing cycle of using the platform.

To use this type of billing flow, the Partner needs to pass **`billAtStart`**: **`false`** when creating the subscription.

### Configuring Subscription Efficiently

There are various parameters to configure during the creation of a subscription like the payment flow, invoice flow, retry strategy, and various other things so that you can run it efficiently. It is very important to understand the meaning of each parameter and how to use it. Some of the important parameters and how they work in different subscription flows are mentioned below.

##### maxQuoteAmount:

This parameter defines the maximum amount that we can auto-debit using the mandate that we have acquired from the customer.

##### dayUntillDue:

For a billing cycle to work properly, the customer needs time to pay any dues that he/she might have. This parameter defines how many days are the customers allowed to pay before the invoice status changes from **`DUE`** to **`OVERDUE`**. Once this happens, the count of **`unpaidInvoices`** increases.

##### maxUnpaidInvoices:

Once the count of **`unpaidInvoices`** is greater than the **`maxUnpaidInvoices`** , the subscription will change the status depending on the value of **`postMaxUnpaidInvoices`**.

##### billingDateAnchor:

Some types of billing cycles need to be started from a specific date of a month. An example of this is Amazon Web Services, where the invoice will always be raised on the 1st day of the month, even if the customer started using the platform on the 15th of the month.

##### maxUnpausedInvoiceDueCount:

This count helps to reactivate a subscription if the subscription was **`PAUSED`** due to **`OVERDUE`** invoices.

##### discount:

Discount object allows to give discount on a subscription, based on the type that is for life time or for a limited period or for one time only.

This API is used to update a specific subscription with a new payment method. Only **`ACTIVE`** or **`PAUSED`** subscriptions can be updated with a new payment method. If a subscription is with **`cancelAtEnd`**: **`true`** then also it is not possible to call this API. Since subscriptions will be automatically canceled at the end of the cycle, adding a new mandate will be useless. For **`PENDING`** subscriptions (which have not been activated once) always use **`payURL`** provided in the [Get Subscription API](#2390e6f2-dabf-4ff5-b1a5-112ab1eb8a4c). For subscriptions that are in terminal states, this API will not work as well. **Note**: Update Mandate by default only allows adding a new mandate for a running subscription. If the partner wants, it is possible to request a mandate with the last due unpaid invoice amount. This way the partner will have a new mandate for auto-debit and an unpaid invoice will be cleared. This feature can be enabled by requesting the administrative team.

item.description:
Mandates allow us to auto-debit the amount from the customer's bank account. Mandate transactions can be of ₹ 2 value for subscriptions with **`billAtStart`** marked as **`false`** or the actual amount of subscription value for those with **`billAtStart`** marked as **`true`**.

In India, a mandate needs a minimum of 24 hours from the bank to get permission to initiate auto-debit. So, if on the 1st of the month, we post a request to the bank to ask permission for an auto-debit amount from the customer's bank account, the bank will notify the customer about the due amount, and after 24 hours we will give permission to invoke auto-debit on the customer's bank account and after that we will be able to deduct the amount. So on the 2nd of the month, we will try to deduct the money from the customer's bank account.

A mandate can be canceled by the end-user using his/her banking app. If a customer has cancelled his mandate we push this event to the mandate web-hook configured by the partner. It is a good practice to check periodically if the mandate linked with the subscription is still in **`ACTIVE`** state or not. If not the customer should be prompted to add a new mandate to make sure the subscription remains active. The partner can ask for a new mandate using [Update Mandate API](#b7abc54b-bf8a-4569-8f65-46b613600d47).

This API can be used to raise a refund against a particular invoice. Webhook events will be pushed for any refund status updates. The response of this API is the Refund Transaction created against the invoice. Once the Refund transaction is marked successful, a credit note will be issued. **Get Transaction API** can also be used to check the refund transaction status. **Note**: For Sandbox Environment, the refund will auto-process in 10 seconds after the request initiation.

They will be passed in the webhook and query API as well. | > `meta` Object Parameters | **Parameters** | **Type** | **Description** | | --- | --- | --- | | param1 | String `Optional` | String with a max length of 256 characters. | | param2 | String `Optional` | String with a max length of 256 characters. | | param3 | String `Optional` | String with a max length of 256 characters. |

Parameter
Type
Description

String

item.description:
Transact Bridge provides full billing management for both one-time and subscription-based invoices.

For the one-time payments, invoices are generated for the paid transactions. For the subscription-based flow, invoices get created on a pre-defined frequency in the `DRAFT` status. We send a webhook once the invoice is created in `DRAFT` status and then the partner approves the invoice using Approve Invoice API which converts that invoice to `DUE` status.

request.description:
This API can be used to raise a refund against a particular invoice. Webhook events will be pushed for any refund status updates. **Get Transaction API** can also be used to check the refund status.

Request Parameters

| **Parameter** | **Type** | **Description** | | --- | --- | --- | | invoiceId | String `Mandatory` | invoice ID | | comment | Array of Object `Optional` | Comments for future use | | referenceId | String `Optional` | Partner's reference ID | | mode | String `Optional` | Mode of payment |

Response Parameters

Parameter
Type
Description
status
String
The response from the API
sub_status
String
Used for internal purposes only
data
Object
Invoice data

request.description:
This API is used to create a unique payment link with a specific amount, that can be shared with a customer. Once the user pays the requested amount through the selected payment channel, the partner gets a webhook call for a successful credit of the same session. A session can accommodate multiple transactions. Each transaction is considered an attempt to make payments with valid credentials. A customer can try to make a payment using different payment methods in different payment attempts. Using the [Get Session API](https://), all the transactions created during a session can also be found and each transaction status can be found using [Get Transaction API](https://).

Request Parameters

Parameter
Type
Description
email
String Mandatory
Customer's email. Require this to send an invoice.
phoneNo
String Optional
The customer's Indian phone number in the format of
+91-XXXXXXXX
taxIdentity
String Optional
PAN number of the customer
gstIdentity
String Optional
If the customer is business type then GST can be used so that the business can get input credits.
customerId
String Optional
Validator: MongoId
If the customer is already created, use customerId and then email will be optional
name
String Optional
Validator: AllowedString
Name of the customer
quoteCurrCode
String Mandatory
Enum: USD, EURO, INR
Fiat currency for which request needs to be initiated.
returnUrl
String Optional
This URL is needed to redirect the user after the session is completed. The end customer will be redirected to this URL with the status of the session.
pendingUrl
String Optional
This URL is needed to redirect the user after the session is in CREATED or STARTED status
failedUrl
String Optional
This URL is needed to redirect the user after the session is in EXPIRED status
successUrl
String Optional
This URL is needed to redirect the user after the session is in CLOSED status
referenceId
String Optional
Validator: ReferenceId
This is a partner ID to map a billing session with their generated ID. This cannot be the same for two sessions.
userTaxId
String Optional
TaxId created by the partner. This allows us to tax the product correctly. For example: Partners can have two different userTaxId one for 18% GST and the other for 5% GST.
shipTo
Object Optional
Shipping Details of the Customer
billTo
Object Optional
Billing Details of the customer for the invoice.
sameAsBilling
Boolean Optional
To make the shipping address the same as the billing address
payMethod
Array of String Optional
Enum: UPI, NB, CC, DC
If the payMethod parameter is sent by the partner then on the payment page for the customer only that payment method will be visible to the user.
redirectionType
String Optional
Enum: SAME_PAGE, NEW_PAGE
Depending on the redirection type value, the user will either be redirected to a new page for payment or to the same window of the product page. Default value is NEW_PAGE.
items
Array of Object Mandatory
Items the customer wants to buy.
expiryDate
Date Optional
The UTC date at which the session link will expire. If this is not passed by the partner, then the link will expire after 24 hours. This is the expiry of the link if a customer attempts the payment at the last minute, then the link will not expire until all the payment attempts transactions are not in the terminal state.
Note: Minimum 10 minutes in the future will be accepted as a valid expiryDate from the time of session creation.
invoice
Object Optional
Invoice configuration for the product sold in this billing session.
serviceStart
Date Optional
If the product is for a specific period, then the invoice created for the customer will have this parameter.
serviceEnd
Date Optional
If the product is for a specific period, then the invoice created for the customer will have this parameter.
meta
Object Optional
To add meta params in the session. They will be passed in the webhook and query API as well.
**Note:** 1. If `sameAsBilling` the parameter value is set as `true` then the invoice will have the same shipping and billing address. 2. `pendingUrl` , `successUrl` , `failedUrl` are all mandatory if `returnUrl` is not passed. > **`billTo`** & **`shipTo`** Object Parameters | **Parameters** | **Type** | **Description** | | --- | --- | --- | | name | String `Optional`
Validator: `AllowedString` | Name of the customer | | line1 | String `Optional`
Validator: `Address` | Address Line 1 of the customer | | line2 | String `Optional`
Validator: `Address` | Address Line 2 of the customer | | city | String `Optional`
Validator: `AllowedString` | City of the customer | | postalCode | String `Optional` | Pin code of the customer. Only Indian codes are accepted. | | state | String `Optional`
Enum: `State` | State of the customer address | | country | String `Optional`
Enum: **`INDIA`** | Country of the customer. Only India is accepted right now. | > **`items`** Array of Object Parameters | **Parameters** | **Type** | **Description** | | --- | --- | --- | | name | String `Mandatory`
Validator: `AllowedString` | Name of the product the customer wants to buy. | | quantity | String `Mandatory` | Number of products the customer wants to buy. | | amount | String `Mandatory` | Price of the product. | | description | String `Optional`
Validator: `MultiLineDescription` | Description of the product. | > `meta` Object Parameters | **Parameters** | **Type** | **Description** | | --- | --- | --- | | param1 | String `Optional` | String with max length of 256 characters. | | param2 | String `Optional` | String with max length of 256 characters. | | param3 | String `Optional` | String with max length of 256 characters. | > **`invoice`** Object Parameters | **Parameters** | **Type** | **Description** | | --- | --- | --- | | issueDate | Date `Optional` | Issue date of the invoice | | dueDate | Date `Optional` | Due date of the invoice | **Note:** Description in the items object allows HTML syntax for customer view so that the description on the product page is better from a UI perspective. We break and show the description like unlisted items.

Response Parameters

Parameter
Type
Description
status
String
The response from the API
sub_status
String
Used for internal purposes only
data
Object
Contains the sessionId and URL.
**Note:** **`returnUrl`** will be a **GET** redirection with status, billingSessionId, and referenceId values passed in the query params. Partner can then use [Get Session API](https://docs.transactbridge.com/#49db9313-bb0c-4732-a59d-b9ce0fec6217) to get the full details of that session.

item.description:
Products define the specific goods or services the partner is offering to its customers. Products can be physical goods, digital goods, SaaS subscriptions, etc. The products also have compliance data embedded within them, which allows proper tax calculations per item based.  
  
Products are not mandatory for any kind of partner, but using products to create a catalogue and then using those product objects to create sessions and subscriptions allows efficient operations.

item.name: Coupons

request.description:
This API endpoint is used to verify whether a coupon is allowed and how to apply it. When a customer enters a coupon code on the checkout page, this API endpoint will be used to validate the coupon code. Coupons are of three types: **`LIMITED_PERIOD`**, **`ONETIME`**, and **`FOREVER`**. They can be used to give a discount based on a percentage or a fixed amount. Please check examples for the same.

For percentage-based **`discountPcnt`** is required, for fixed amount-based **`discountQuoteAmount`** is required. Also for the limited period-based coupons, **`period`** and **`periodUnit`** both are required.

item.description:
The coupon system allows the partner the flexibility to use their coupon store and let their customer apply coupon codes at the Transact Bridge checkout page. When a customer applies a coupon, we call the [Coupon Verify API ](#b9d75ad2-d7ad-42b9-9aa1-79272fae869e) configured by the partner. Partner can evaluate the coupon code at their end and check if the. coupon code is valid and respond accordingly. Partners can allow coupons with limited period applicability for both percentage-based or fixed amount-based. The response excepted from the partner is mentioned in the example of the Coupon Verify API

To configure the Coupon Verify API endpoint, the partner can log in to their account go to the **Account Settings page**, and configure the endpoint there.

item.description:
Partner will receive events on their configured server URL. These webhooks are POST requests originating from our server. Authorization headers will also be sent so the partner can authenticate that the request is created by the original web server only. Merchants will configure this URL in the panel to start receiving webhook events. For documentation and reading purposes, [https://configuredURLFromBroker.com](https://) URL is used. But, in the production environment, the configured URL of the partner will be used.

Callback Response

Partners should respond to the callback with a JSON response `{"ack": true}` If the merchant fails to send the response we will send again the callback. The maximum number of attempts by us will be 3. If the partner fails to acknowledge the callback in three attempts, then there will be no subsequent callback. The partner can create a callback from the panel

Signature Verification

We use the same method to create the signature that is used in the authorization headers.
